claim_status: No-claim ownership and control source observations only; these sources are not qualified-backup evidence, access approval, branch-protection proof, two-person-control evidence, release authority, production authority, or build-readiness closure.

sources.title: The NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.observation: The CSF 2.0 Govern function includes organizational context, roles/responsibilities/authorities, policy, oversight, and supply-chain governance outcomes.
sources.control_consequence: Turing ownership records must identify accountable roles, authority boundaries, oversight, and scope-specific risk; CSF outcomes do not name Turing owners, backups, or release authorities.

sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5, AC-5 Separation of Duties
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.observation: NIST SP 800-53 includes AC-5 separation of duties, requiring organizations to identify duties requiring separation and define access authorizations that support it.
sources.control_consequence: Two-person-control evidence must map separated duties to actual access authorizations and action records; a policy statement or two names without independent access and review evidence is insufficient.

sources.title: About code owners
sources.observation: GitHub CODEOWNERS routes review requests for matching paths; code owners and teams need repository write access, and code-owner approval can be required through repository settings.
sources.control_consequence: Turing must reconcile path patterns, precedence, team membership, write access, and CODEOWNERS-file ownership with qualified-person records; routing does not prove competence, availability, independence, or backup coverage.

sources.title: About protected branches
sources.observation: GitHub protected branches can require approving reviews, status checks, signed commits, restricted pushes, and controls on bypassing protections.
sources.control_consequence: Evidence must capture the effective rule, review count, code-owner requirement, stale-review behavior, status checks, push restrictions, deletion/force-push settings, and bypass actors; a rule's existence is not an independent control assessment.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for protected branches
sources.observation: GitHub's protected-branch API exposes required review counts, code-owner review, stale-review dismissal, last-push approval, dismissal restrictions, and bypass allowances as inspectable configuration fields.
sources.control_consequence: A future reconciliation packet should retain an authenticated effective-configuration capture and digest it with repository identity and timestamp; API shape alone is not current repository configuration or proof that a control was exercised.

unsupported: No qualified backup owner is named or verified by this manifest; current PB-019 primary-only/null-backup status remains unchanged.
No authenticated repository access capture, effective branch-rule capture, CODEOWNERS exercise, two-person-control exercise, succession drill, or independent owner review exists.
No release, signing, disclosure, legal, incident-closure, profile-migration, production, or build-readiness authority follows from these sources.

No owner coverage, backup coverage, least-privilege, branch-protection, separation-of-duties, two-person-control, production, Chrome-class, performance, or implementation claim follows from this manifest.
